1→name: Wayback Preserve New URLs
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→    paths:
     7→      - 'SOURCES/**'
     8→      - 'GOVERNMENTS/**'
     9→      - 'TOPICS/**'
    10→
    11→jobs:
    12→  preserve:
    13→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    14→    permissions:
    15→      contents: write
    16→
    17→    steps:
    18→      - name: Checkout vault
    19→        uses: actions/checkout@v6
    20→        with:
    21→          fetch-depth: 2  # Need previous commit to diff
    22→
    23→      - name: Setup vault environment
    24→        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-vault
    25→
    26→      - name: Extract new URLs from changed notes
    27→        id: extract
    28→        run: |
    29→          # Get files changed in this push
    30→          git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD -- '*.md' > /tmp/changed_files.txt
    31→          echo "Changed files:"
    32→          cat /tmp/changed_files.txt
    33→
    34→          # Extract URL fields from changed files
    35→          python3 - << 'PYTHON'
    36→          import re, sys
    37→          from pathlib import Path
    38→
    39→          changed = Path("/tmp/changed_files.txt").read_text().splitlines()
    40→          urls = []
    41→          for f in changed:
    42→              p = Path(f)
    43→              if not p.exists() or p.suffix != ".md":
    44→                  continue
    45→              content = p.read_text(errors="replace")
    46→              m = re.search(r"^URL:\s*(.+)$", content, re.MULTILINE)
    47→              if m:
    48→                  url = m.group(1).strip()
    49→                  if url and "web.archive.org" not in url and url != "null":
    50→                      urls.append(url)
    51→
    52→          Path("/tmp/new_urls.txt").write_text("\n".join(urls))
    53→          print(f"Found {len(urls)} new URLs to preserve")
    54→          for u in urls:
    55→              print(f"  {u}")
    56→          PYTHON
    57→
    58→      - name: Submit to Save Page Now
    59→        run: |
    60→          python3 - << 'PYTHON'
    61→          import urllib.request, urllib.error, time
    62→          from pathlib import Path
    63→
    64→          UA = "IDAHO-VAULT/1.0 (Idaho Reports journalism archive; github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT)"
    65→          urls_file = Path("/tmp/new_urls.txt")
    66→          if not urls_file.exists():
    67→              print("No URLs file found, skipping")
    68→              exit(0)
    69→
    70→          urls = [u.strip() for u in urls_file.read_text().splitlines() if u.strip()]
    71→          if not urls:
    72→              print("No new URLs to preserve")
    73→              exit(0)
    74→
    75→          print(f"Submitting {len(urls)} URLs to Save Page Now...")
    76→          results = []
    77→          for url in urls:
    78→              save_url = f"https://web.archive.org/save/{url}"
    79→              try:
    80→                  req = urllib.request.Request(
    81→                      save_url,
    82→                      method="GET",
    83→                      headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"}
    84→                  )
    85→                  with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
    86→                      loc = resp.headers.get("Content-Location", "")
    87→                      archived = f"https://web.archive.org{loc}" if loc else save_url
    88→                      print(f"  ✅ {url[:70]}")
    89→                      print(f"     → {archived}")
    90→                      results.append((url, archived, True))
    91→              except Exception as e:
    92→                  print(f"  ❌ {url[:70]}: {e}")
    93→                  results.append((url, None, False))
    94→              time.sleep(5)  # SPN rate limit
    95→
    96→          # Write preservation log
    97→          from pathlib import Path
    98→          from datetime import datetime, timezone
    99→          import os
   100→
   101→          date_str = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
   102→          admin = Path("!")
   103→          admin.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
   104→          log_path = admin / f"wayback-preserve-{date_str}.md"
   105→
   106→          lines = [
   107→              f"# Wayback Preservation Log — {date_str}",
   108→              "",
   109→              f"URLs submitted to Save Page Now on push to main.",
   110→              "",
   111→              "| URL | Archived | Status |",
   112→              "|---|---|---|",
   113→          ]
   114→          for url, archived, ok in results:
   115→              status = "✅" if ok else "❌"
   116→              arc = f"[snapshot]({archived})" if archived else "failed"
   117→              lines.append(f"| {url[:70]} | {arc} | {status} |")
   118→
   119→          log_path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
   120→          print(f"\nLog written to {log_path}")
   121→          PYTHON
   122→
   123→      - name: Commit preservation log
   124→        run: |
   125→          git add "!/"

   127→            git commit -m "wayback preservation log $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→state_manager.py — Minimal read/write/update interface for swarm run state.
     4→
     5→Usage:
     6→    python swarm/tools/state_manager.py                          # Print current run state
     7→    python swarm/tools/state_manager.py read                     # Print current run state
     8→    python swarm/tools/state_manager.py update "SECTION" "content"  # Update a section
     9→    python swarm/tools/state_manager.py snapshot                 # Archive current state
    10→    python swarm/tools/state_manager.py archive                  # Archive current state (alias)
    11→"""
    12→
    13→import sys
    14→import shutil
    15→from pathlib import Path
    16→from datetime import datetime
    17→
    18→BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    19→STATE_DIR = BASE_DIR / "state"
    20→ARCHIVE_DIR = STATE_DIR / "archive"
    21→RUN_STATE = STATE_DIR / "run_state.md"
    22→
    23→
    24→def read_run_state():
    25→    if not RUN_STATE.exists():
    26→        return ""
    27→    return RUN_STATE.read_text()
    28→
    29→
    30→def write_run_state(content: str):
    31→    timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
    32→    # Strip any existing LAST UPDATED section before appending new one
    33→    lines = content.split("\n")
    34→    cleaned = []
    35→    skip = False
    36→    for line in lines:
    37→        if line.strip() == "# LAST UPDATED":
    38→            skip = True
    39→            continue
    40→        if skip and line.startswith("# "):
    41→            skip = False
    42→        if not skip:
    43→            cleaned.append(line)
    44→
    45→    # Remove trailing blank lines
    46→    while cleaned and cleaned[-1].strip() == "":
    47→        cleaned.pop()
    48→
    49→    cleaned.append("")
    50→    cleaned.append("# LAST UPDATED")
    51→    cleaned.append(timestamp)
    52→    cleaned.append("")
    53→
    54→    RUN_STATE.write_text("\n".join(cleaned))
    55→
    56→
    57→def update_section(section_title: str, new_content: str):
    58→    text = read_run_state()
    59→    # Parse into list of (title, body) tuples
    60→    sections = []
    61→    current_title = None
    62→    current_body = []
    63→
    64→    for line in text.split("\n"):
    65→        if line.startswith("# "):
    66→            if current_title is not None:
    67→                sections.append((current_title, "\n".join(current_body)))
    68→            current_title = line[2:].strip()
    69→            current_body = []
    70→        else:
    71→            current_body.append(line)
    72→
    73→    if current_title is not None:
    74→        sections.append((current_title, "\n".join(current_body)))
    75→
    76→    # Replace or append
    77→    found = False
    78→    result_parts = []
    79→    for title, body in sections:
    80→        if title == section_title:
    81→            result_parts.append(f"# {title}\n{new_content}\n")
    82→            found = True
    83→        else:
    84→            result_parts.append(f"# {title}\n{body}")
    85→
    86→    if not found:
    87→        result_parts.append(f"# {section_title}\n{new_content}\n")
    88→
    89→    write_run_state("\n".join(result_parts))
    90→
    91→
    92→def snapshot():
    93→    if not RUN_STATE.exists():
    94→        print("No run_state.md to snapshot.")
    95→        return
    96→
    97→    ARCHIVE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    98→    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S")
